name: King Abdulaziz Air Base
hardening: Hardened aircraft shelters; Patriot missile defense
description: Major RSAF installation near Dhahran in the Eastern Province, directly across the Persian Gulf from Iran. Hosts F-15SA and Typhoon squadrons defending Saudi Arabia's critical oil infrastructure at Abqaiq and Ras Tanura. Frontline base in any Iran scenario.

name: King Khalid Air Base
hardening: Hardened aircraft shelters; THAAD and Patriot missile defense
description: Critical RSAF installation near Khamis Mushait in southwestern Saudi Arabia. Primary launch point for Saudi coalition airstrikes during Yemen conflict. Targeted multiple times by Houthi drones and ballistic missiles, leading to THAAD and Patriot deployment.

name: Al Minhad Air Base
hardening: Hardened aircraft shelters; air defense coverage
description: Major UAE military airfield 24 km south of Dubai. Key Western coalition logistics and staging hub hosting forces from Australia, France, and other partners. Modern infrastructure and long runway make it important for any coalition air operations in the Gulf.

name: Cairo West Air Base
description: One of Egyptian Air Force's largest installations, 50 km west of Cairo. Hosts frontline F-16 and Rafale fighter squadrons. Long runway accommodates all aircraft types. Used as staging base for joint exercises with the US.

name: Berenice Military Base
units: Egyptian Navy Southern Red Sea Command
equipment: Ambassador MK III fast missile craft
description: Egypt's newest military installation (2020) on the Red Sea coast near Sudan. Joint naval-air base extending power projection into the southern Red Sea and toward Bab el-Mandeb. Reflects Egypt's growing concern over Red Sea security and Houthi shipping attacks.
